name: Alive
description_short: Alive Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative offering checking, savings, loans, and credit products with a focus on financial wellness and community involvement.
description_long:
Alive Credit Union operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned cooperative serving healthcare workers, law enforcement, city employees, and local communities. The organization emphasizes financial wellness as its core mission, positioning itself around the tagline 'Inspiring Financial Wellness' and offering comprehensive financial education resources alongside traditional banking products.

Alive provides a full suite of consumer financial services including multiple checking account tiers (Free Checking, Interest Checking, Fresh Start Checking, 55+ Senior Checking, Teen Checking), savings accounts, certificates of deposit, money market accounts, and retirement accounts (IRAs and HSAs). On the lending side, they offer personal loans (including Credit Builder Loans, Easy Cash Plus Loans, Medical Loans, and Tactical Loans), auto loans, recreational vehicle loans, home mortgages, home equity lines of credit, and student loans. They also issue Visa debit and credit cards, provide business banking services, and offer merchant card processing.

Alive distinguishes itself through specialized programs targeting specific member groups, including a Pathfinder Teen Club for youth financial education, dedicated 55+ senior checking, and police/city employee membership tracks. They provide financial wellness coaching, scholarship programs, and community involvement initiatives beyond standard banking. The credit union operates a nationwide shared branching network, surcharge-free ATM access, and supports multiple service channels including online banking, mobile banking with mobile deposit, telephone banking, and Spanish-language support.

Alive is a legitimate NCUA-insured credit union with a physical headquarters location (9790 Touchton Road, Jacksonville area based on context), established community presence, and membership eligibility tied to specific employer groups and communities. The organization demonstrates transparency through annual membership meetings and maintains investment in member resources and financial education.
services: Free Checking accounts with no minimum balance or monthly fees
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Money Market Accounts
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Credit Builder Loans for credit establishment
Personal loans including Medical Loans, Easy Cash Plus Loans, and Tactical Loans
Auto loans and Recreational Vehicle loans
Home mortgage loans, home equity loans, and home improvement loans
Business checking, savings, and business loan services
Financial Wellness Coaching and educational programs
Mobile banking with mobile deposit and online bill pay
pros: Multiple checking account options including Free Checking with no minimum balance or monthly fees
Specialized Teen Checking and Pathfinder Teen Club program for youth financial education
Credit Builder Loans and Medical Loans specifically designed for members rebuilding credit or managing healthcare costs
Financial Wellness Coaching and comprehensive financial wellness programs available to members
Nationwide Shared Branching network and surcharge-free ATM access for convenience
Spanish-language services ('Hablamos tu idioma') for non-English speaking members
Wide range of loan products including recreational vehicle loans, home equity lines, and home improvement loans
cons: Membership eligibility appears restricted to specific employer groups (healthcare, law enforcement, city employees, local communities) rather than open to general public
Limited information on specific APR ranges, interest rates, or fee structures available on the website
No mention of mobile app ratings, online review scores, or third-party ratings visible on the website
No information provided about loan approval timelines or application process details
Website does not clearly detail membership requirements or eligibility verification process upfront
best_for: Healthcare workers, law enforcement officers, and city government employees seeking credit union membership
Teens and young adults interested in building financial literacy through dedicated youth programs
Individuals seeking credit builder loans or medical-specific lending options
Members prioritizing financial wellness coaching and community-focused banking relationships
diagnosis: Alive Credit Union is best for employees in healthcare, law enforcement, and municipal government who meet membership eligibility requirements and seek comprehensive banking with integrated financial wellness support. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to specific employer and community groups rather than being open to the general public, requiring verification of eligibility before account opening.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Alive — Credit Union in Jacksonville, FL | CreditDoc
meta_description: Alive — NCUA-insured credit union in Jacksonville, FL. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
